description: Legal fragment, either a will or something to do with the disposition of the property of a certain Ibn al-Raqyan (בן אלרקיין) who is now deceased. Mentions a wife; 10 dinars; Yosef Av Bet Din; [...] b. Meshullam; and arriving in Egypt or Fustat. Signed by ʿEli b. Yaḥyā ha-Kohen (ca. late 11th century). Verso also preserves a filing note: "testament of Abū Naṣr the in-law of Ṣedaqa al-Ramlī al-Uṭrūsh."
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בהספרייה הבריטית.
